title: Optimized crosslinkers for trapping a target on a substrate
abstract: The presently-disclosed subject matter relates to crosslinkers, compositions, and methods for trapping a target of interest on a substrate of interest. The methods may be used to inhibit and treat pathogen infection and provide contraception. The methods may be used to trap or separate particles and other substances. The subject matter further relates to methods of identifying and preparing optimal crosslinkers and methods for manipulating targets of interest.
claims:
Claims We claim: 1. A method of selecting a crosslmker for trapping a target of interest on a substrate of interest, comprising: (a) determining an optimal target binding affinity and substrate binding affinity for the target of interest and the substrate of interest using a mathematical model; (b) measuring the target binding affinity and substrate binding affinity of one or more crosslinkers; and (c) selecting a crosslinker that substantially matches the optimal target binding affinity and substrate binding affinity determined in step (a). 2. A method of selecting a crosslinker for trapping a target of interest on a substrate of interest, comprising: (a) determining an optimal target binding affinity and substrate binding affinity for the target of interest and the substrate of interest using a mathematical model; (b) altering the target binding affinity and/or substrate binding affinity of one or more crosslinkers; (c) measuring the target binding affinity and substrate binding affinity of the one or more altered crosslinkers; and (d) selecting an altered crosslinker that substantially matches the optimal target binding affinity and substrate binding affinity determined in step (a). 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the mathematical model includes two or more factors selected from the fraction of time the crosslinker spends associated with the substrate, the rate of binding of the crosslinker to the target, the crosslinker concentration, the size of the target, the diffusivity of the target, the diffusivity of the crosslinker, and the number of target binding sites. 4. A method of selecting a crosslinker for trapping a target of interest on a substrate of interest, comprising: (a) measuring the target binding affinity and substrate binding affinity of one or more crosslinkers; and 

 (b) selecting a crosslinker that substantially matches a predetermined optimal target binding affinity and substrate binding affinity. 5. A method of selecting a crosslinker for trapping a target of interest on a substrate of interest, comprising: (a) altering the target binding affinity and/or substrate binding affinity of one or more crosslinkers; (b) measuring the target binding affinity and substrate binding affinity of the one or more crosslinkers; and (c) selecting an altered crosslinker that substantially matches a predetermined optimal target binding affinity and substrate binding affinity. 6. The method of any one of claims 1 -5, wherein the target is a pathogen or a sperm cell. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the pathogen is a virus. 8. The method of claim 6, wherein the pathogen is a bacterium. 9. The method of claim 6, wherein the pathogen is a fungus. 10. The method of claim 6, wherein the pathogen is a parasite. 11. The method of any one of claims 1 -5, wherein the target is a particle. 12. The method of claim 11 , wherein the particle is a microparticle or a nanoparticle. 13. The method of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the substrate of interest is mucin and the crosslinker traps the target in mucus. 14. The method of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the substrate of interest is a biopolymer and the crosslinker traps the target on the biopolymer. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the biopolymer is extracellular matrix, laminin, collagen, actin or fibronectin. 16. The method of any one of claims 1-15, wherein the crosslinker is an antibody or an antibody fragment or derivative. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the crosslinker binds an epitope on the target. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the epitope is a non-neutralizing epitope. 19. The method of claim 17, wherein the epitope is a neutralizing epitope. 20. The method of any one of claims 17-19, wherein the epitope is a conserved epitope. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein the conserved epitope is a non-neutralizing epitope. 22. The method of claim 20, wherein the conserved epitope is a neutralizing epitope. 23. The method of any one of claims 16-22, wherein the crosslinker is selected from IgG, IgA, IgM, or a fragment or derivative thereof. 24. The method of any one of claims 16-23, wherein the crosslinker is IgG or a fragment or derivative thereof. 25. The method of any one of claims 16-24, wherein at least one Fc region of the antibody is preserved. 26. The method of any one of claims 16-25, wherein the antibody is a polyclonal antibody. 27. The method of any one of claims 16-25, wherein the antibody is a monoclonal antibody. 28. The method of any one of claims 16-25, wherein the antibody is a combination of monoclonal antibodies. 29. A crosslinker identified by the method of any one of claims 1-28. 30. The crosslinker of claim 29, which is an antibody or an antibody fragment or derivative. 31. A crosslinker for trapping a target of interest on a substrate of interest, wherein the crosslinker associates with the substrate of interest at least about 10% of the time but less than 100% of the time, and has a rate of binding to the target of interest greater than about 10 "4 M ' V 1 . 32. The crosslinker of claim 31 , wherein the crosslinker associates with the substrate of interest about 75% of the time. 33. The crosslinker of claim 31 or 32, wherein the target of interest is a pathogen or a sperm cell. 34. The crosslinker of claim 33, wherein the pathogen is a virus. 35. The crosslinker of claim 33, wherein the pathogen is a bacterium. 36. The crosslinker of claim 33, wherein the pathogen is a fungus. 37. The crosslinker of claim 33, wherein the pathogen is a parasite. 38. The crosslinker of claim 31 or 32, wherein the target is a particle. 39. The crosslinker of claim 38, wherein the particle is a microparticle or a nanoparticle. 40. The crosslinker of any one of claims 31-39, wherein the crosslinker is an antibody or an antibody fragment or derivative. 41. The crosslinker of any one of claims 31-40, wherein the crosslinker binds an epitope on the target. 42. The crosslinker of claim 41, wherein the epitope is a non-neutralizing epitope. 43. The crosslinker of claim 41 , wherein the epitope is a neutralizing epitope. 44. The crosslinker of claim 43, wherein the antibody has trapping potency at a sub-neutralization dose. 45. The crosslinker of any one of claims 41-44, wherein the epitope is a conserved epitope. 46. The crosslinker of claim 45, wherein the conserved epitope is a non- neutralizing epitope. 47. The crosslinker of claim 45, wherein the conserved epitope is a neutralizing epitope. 48. The crosslinker of claim 47, wherein the antibody has trapping potency at a sub-neutralization dose. 49. The crosslinker of any one of claims 40-48, wherein the antibody is selected from IgG, IgA, IgM, or a fragment or derivative thereof. 50. The crosslinker of any one of claims 40-49, wherein the antibody is IgG or a fragment or derivative thereof. 51. The crosslinker of any one of claims 40-50, wherein at least one Fc region is preserved. 52. The crosslinker of any one of claims 40-51 , wherein the antibody is a polyclonal antibody. 53. The crosslinker of any one of claims 40-51, wherein the antibody is a monoclonal antibody. 54. The crosslinker of any one of claims 40-51 , wherein the antibody is a combination of monoclonal antibodies. 55. A composition comprising the crosslinker of any one of claims 29-54. 56. The composition of claim 55, further comprising a second crosslinker, wherein the second crosslinker specifically binds a second target, wherein said second target is distinct from the target to which the first antibody specifically binds. 57. The composition of claim 55, further comprising a second crosslinker, wherein the second crosslinker specifically binds the same target as the target to which the first crosslinker specifically binds. 58. The composition of claim 57, wherein the first crosslinker and the second crosslinker specifically bind different epitopes on the target. 59. A pharmaceutical composition comprising the crosslinker of any one of claims 29-54 or the composition of any one of claims 55-58 and a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier. 60. A kit comprising the crosslinker of any one of claims 29-54 or the composition of any one of claims 55-59. 61. The kit of claim 60, further comprising a device for administering the crosslinker or composition. 62. The kit of claim 60 or 61, further comprising an additional active agent. 63. A method of trapping a target of interest on a substrate of interest, the method comprising contacting the target of interest with the crosslinker of any one of claims 29-53 or the composition of any one of claims 54-58 in an amount effective to trap the target of interest on the substrate of interest. 64. The method of claim 63, wherein the method is part of a process for separating, purifying, washing, and/or filtering the target of interest. 65. The method of claim 64, wherein the substrate of interest is part of a chromatography, dialysis, or filtration medium. 66. The method of claim 63 , wherein the target of interest is a pathogen or a sperm cell. 67. The method of claim 66, wherein the substrate of interest is mucin and the method comprises trapping the pathogen or sperm cell in mucus. 68. The method of claim 66, wherein the substrate of interest is a biopolymer and the crosslinker traps the target on the biopolymer. 69. The method of claim 68, wherein the biopolymer is extracellular matrix, laminin, collagen, actin or fibronectin. 70. A method of inhibiting an infection by a pathogen or a disease or disorder caused by an infection by a pathogen in a subject in need thereof, comprising administering to a mucosa of the subject the crosslinker of any one of claims 29-54 or the composition of any one of claims 55-59 in an amount effective to inhibit the infection or the disease or disorder caused by an infection; wherein the crosslinker specifically binds the pathogen. 71. A method of treating an infection by a pathogen or a disease or disorder caused by an infection by a pathogen in a subject in need thereof, comprising administering to a mucosa of the subject the crosslinker of any one of claims 29-54 or 

 the composition of any one of claims 55-59 in an amount effective to treat the infection or the disease or disorder caused by the infection; wherein the crosslmker specifically binds the pathogen. 72. The method of claim 70 or 71, wherein the mucosa is selected from an oral mucosa, a nasal mucosa, a lung mucosa, a genital mucosa, an ocular mucosa, a gastrointestinal mucosa, a reproductive tract mucosa, and any combination thereof. 73. A method of providing contraception in a female subject, comprising administering to a mucosa of a reproductive tract of the subject the crosslinker of any one of claims 29-54 or the composition of any one of claims 55-59 in an amount effective to provide contraception, wherein the crosslinker specifically binds a sperm cell. 74. The method of any one of claims 70-73, wherein the composition is administered intranasally, orally, intravaginally, by inhalation, or topically. 75. The method of any one of claims 70-74, wherein the subject is a human. 76. A computer program product comprising: a computer readable storage medium having computer readable code embodied in the medium, the computer code comprising: computer readable code to perform operations to determine an optimal target binding affinity and substrate binding affinity for a target of interest and an substrate of interest using a mathematical model. 77. A computer system, comprising: a processor; and a memory coupled to the processor, the memory comprising computer readable program code embodied therein that, when executed by the processor, causes the processor to perform operations to determine an optimal target binding affinity and substrate binding affinity for a target of interest and an substrate of interest using a mathematical model.
